hypothesis: If solid-state NEB pathways for phase transformations in CsPbI3 and TiO2 are initialized and preoptimized with pretrained equivariant ML surrogates (EquiformerV2 or eSEN) before DFT-based refinement, then the resulting transition states and energy barriers will match pure-DFT NEB results while requiring fewer iterations and less compute time because the ML models provide sufficiently accurate energies, forces, and stresses to generate near-converged pathway images.
